div.head: Mr. Ernest J. Swift of the American Red Cross to the Chief of the Division of Near Eastern Affairs, Department of State (Dulles)
div.p: My Dear Mr. Dulles: Enclosed you will find a cable sent by Mr. Morgenthau from Athens to the American Red Cross, dated November 22, 1923, which may be of interest to the State Department. We have sent him a simple acknowledgment by post.

frus:attachment.head: The Chairman of the Refugee Settlement Commission (Morgenthau) to the Chairman of the American Red Cross (Payne)
frus:attachment.p: Highest appreciation constantly expressed here for recent Red Cross assistance. Greeks implore for its renewal to prevent wholesale starvation this winter. Refugees still unplaced. Our commission prevented by its organic statutes from expending its funds for temporary relief. Greek government financially unable to cope with entire situation hence this emergency. A few million so easily obtained in America will save situation and help Greece in her gallant struggle to absorb one million of its destitute Christian brothers. Greek government has drawn heavily on her resources in permanently settling quarter of million refugees. More than seven hundred thousand penniless sparsely dressed refugees are still inhumanely huddled in theatres, churches, schools, camps. Our commission hopeful accomplishing its task if some noble charity will keep refugees alive until spring.
